master_style: MASTER STYLE: Vibrant, warm children's book illustration in soft digital painting style. Clean outlines with brilliant Caribbean colors — turquoise and deep blue ocean, brilliant white and gold sand, vivid tropical sky, lush greens. Tito the baby sea turtle has big expressive eyes, a small round shell, and tiny cute flippers. The beach setting is a gorgeous Dominican/Caribbean coast. 16:9 landscape format. Style feels bright, clear, and full of tropical wonder. Every image should feel like a perfect sunny beach day, full of adventure and discovery for a tiny brave turtle.
page_prompts: Children's book illustration, 16:9 landscape. A gorgeous Caribbean beach scene — pale gold sand, brilliant turquoise water, bright blue sky, a few palm trees in the background. In the center of the sandy beach sits Tito — a tiny, adorable baby sea turtle with big dark expressive eyes, a small round green-olive shell, and four little flippers. He sits on the warm sand looking around curiously and a little unsure. He is very small — emphasize his smallness against the wide beach. The scene should feel warm, bright, and beautiful. Master style: vibrant children's book illustration, soft digital painting, 16:9.

Children's book illustration, 16:9 landscape. Tito (tiny baby sea turtle, big dark eyes, small round green shell, four little flippers) sits on the golden sand, looking out toward the ocean. The view is from behind/beside Tito so we can see what he sees — the vast, glittering turquoise and blue Caribbean ocean stretching to the horizon, with gentle waves rolling in. The ocean looks enormous compared to little Tito. His expression is wide-eyed wonder — a little intimidated, a little enchanted. The water is crystal clear, turquoise near shore fading to deep blue further out. Master style: vibrant children's book illustration, soft digital painting, 16:9.

Children's book illustration, 16:9 landscape. The shoreline of a beautiful Caribbean beach. A gentle wave rolls in toward the shore in a smooth, clear curve — you can see the bright turquoise water through the wave face. The wave reaches the sand, spreads out as white foam, then the water pulls back leaving the sand dark and wet. Tito (tiny baby sea turtle, big dark eyes, small round green shell) watches this from the dry sand nearby, head tilting to follow the wave in and then back out, clearly fascinated. Maybe show the wave sequence as one image with two wave positions. Master style: vibrant children's book illustration, soft digital painting, 16:9.

Children's book illustration, 16:9 landscape. Tito (tiny baby sea turtle, big dark eyes, small round green shell) walks slowly and carefully across the wet sand toward the water's edge. He takes small, careful steps with his four little flippers. The waves have retreated and the sand ahead is dark and wet, with tiny rivulets of water. The turquoise ocean is right there, just ahead. Tito's expression is brave but cautious — determined, but taking it one step at a time. His footprints trail behind him in the sand. Master style: vibrant children's book illustration, soft digital painting, 16:9.

Children's book illustration, 16:9 landscape. Close-up view at the water's edge. Tito (tiny baby sea turtle, big dark expressive eyes, small round green shell) stands at the very edge of the Caribbean water. He carefully extends ONE tiny front flipper into the shallow, crystal-clear water — just dipping it in. The water is perfectly clear, you can see the sandy bottom. Tito's face shows a mix of surprise and delight at the cool feeling. Maybe small ripples spread from where his flipper touches the water. His body is still half on the sand, half leaning toward the water. Master style: vibrant children's book illustration, soft digital painting, 16:9.

Children's book illustration, 16:9 landscape. At the shallow water's edge, Tito (tiny baby sea turtle, big dark eyes, small green shell) stands at the shoreline, flipper still in the water, when a small bright tropical fish (orange and white stripes, or blue and yellow) swims right up to Tito in the shallow clear water. The fish has a big friendly smile and looks directly at Tito with a happy expression. Tito's eyes are WIDE with happy surprise — a new friend! The water is crystal clear and you can see the sandy bottom. The moment feels magical and encouraging. Master style: vibrant children's book illustration, soft digital painting, 16:9.

Children's book illustration, 16:9 landscape. Tito (tiny baby sea turtle, big dark eyes, small round green shell) is now IN the water — up to his middle, wading into the Caribbean shallows. His expression shows determination and courage — cheeks puffed slightly as if holding a big breath, eyes focused ahead. The crystal-clear turquoise water is around him, gently lapping. The sandy bottom is visible below him. He is going DEEPER, one brave step at a time. The sunlight makes the water shimmer beautifully around him. Master style: vibrant children's book illustration, soft digital painting, 16:9.

Children's book illustration, 16:9 landscape. Tito (tiny baby sea turtle, big dark eyes, small round green shell) is SWIMMING! His four little flippers kick and paddle through the clear turquoise Caribbean water. He is underwater-level or just at the surface, moving through the water with clear motion. His face shows PURE JOY — wide eyes sparkling, a big smile. Motion lines or small bubbles trail from his flippers to show movement. The water is beautiful turquoise, sun streaming through from above making golden patterns. This is the DISCOVERY PAGE — Tito can swim! Master style: vibrant children's book illustration, soft digital painting, 16:9.

Children's book illustration, 16:9 landscape. Tito (tiny baby sea turtle, big dark eyes, small round green shell) floats or swims in the beautiful turquoise Caribbean water, surrounded by the cool clear ocean. His eyes are half-closed in bliss, a peaceful and deeply contented expression. The water around him is crystalline, with beautiful light patterns filtering down. Maybe small colorful fish swim nearby. The mood is serene, peaceful, and deeply happy — Tito has found his home element. The water feels cool, clear, and perfect. Master style: vibrant children's book illustration, soft digital painting, 16:9.

Children's book illustration, 16:9 landscape. Tito (tiny baby sea turtle, big dark eyes, small round green shell) is swimming when suddenly a BIG wave looms up ahead — it's huge compared to little Tito, curling and rolling forward fast. Tito's eyes go WIDE with surprise and slight alarm, but also a hint of excitement. The wave is impressive but not terrifying — it's a big, beautiful wave curling toward him. The turquoise water churns with energy. Tito looks tiny and brave against the oncoming wave. Master style: vibrant children's book illustration, soft digital painting, 16:9.

Children's book illustration, 16:9 landscape. Tito (tiny baby sea turtle, big dark eyes, small round green shell) is riding the crest of the big wave, arms out like he's flying, being carried fast toward the shore on top of the white churning wave. His face shows ABSOLUTE DELIGHT — laughing, thrilled, arms spread wide. The wave crests around him in beautiful white foam and turquoise water. The beach and palm trees are visible ahead getting closer fast. This is the most exciting, joyful moment — Tito surfing! Master style: vibrant children's book illustration, soft digital painting, 16:9.

Children's book illustration, 16:9 landscape. A beautiful Caribbean beach and ocean scene. Tito (tiny baby sea turtle, big dark eyes, small round green shell) swims confidently and happily through the clear turquoise water near the colorful reef or in the bright shallows, surrounded by small friendly tropical fish. He is completely at home — swimming with easy, confident flipper strokes, a big happy smile, eyes sparkling. The ocean around him is gorgeous — turquoise, clear, with sunlight patterns. The beach is visible in the background, warm and bright. Everything says: THIS IS TITO'S HAPPY PLACE. Master style: vibrant children's book illustration, soft digital painting, 16:9.
